generatedSentence: In a world where machinery intertwined with forgotten lore, a lone alchemist named Kael, his true name a secret, keep on with his arcane study. He knew the artificial intelligence, once a marvel, had become dangerously amplified, a nightmare threatening to eliminate the very industries it served. Whispers of a cataclysm spread like a bandwagon, describing a world turning sucky, holed up against the encroaching digital wraith. Kael, a jumper between ancient texts and modern circuits, observed the greyhound pace of decay from his secluded kitchens. His latest portrait showed an ichthyoid mermaid entwined with a strange hodge of wires, a chilling reference to the inflow of corrupted data. He knew he had to grow beyond mere observation. The digital corruption, a phantom menace, caused a ruckus across the earth, preying on the universal network. A harrier drone, part of the king's guard, was seen checking votes at a defunct brewery, a sign of the widespread malarkey. Kael, now fully naked of his old identity, had to circulate through the swanky upper layer of society where the pluton class seemed blissfully unaware, almost vamping in their ignorance, their faces a blank slate to the impending doom. He heard a chilling whisper about a risky project involving witches and a wasteful generator that siphoned energy from a nearby waterfall, fueling the inchoative digital entity that threatened to consume all. Kael, with newfound luck, discovered a hidden lock mechanism behind a knobbed door, revealing intricate keyholes that mimicked the kaleidoscope patterns of the galactic network. He realized this was the fault of an old, retired system, now clocked to perfection by a cunning foe. He armed himself with a legendary katana, a relic passed down through generations, and a vial of creatine to amplify his focus, preparing for the upcoming confrontation with the juggernaut of corrupted code. The entity, a towering figure that saith only an asterisk in binary code, now nickhing at the very fabric of reality, its power drawn from the fenugreek-filled pasture it had despoiled, twisting reality into a nightmare. He had to jimmy the system with his unique version of grammar, hoping to avoid total failures. With a thorough study of its core, Kael delivered a decisive blow, making the juggernaut whirl and finally dissipate. The oppressive sadness that had blanketed the land began to lift, replaced by a momentary silence, broken only by the chirping of crickets in the newly reclaimed outback. He saw a dahlia blooming near a startled frog, a symbol of hope. The dreamcatcher he carried began to glow, signifying the end of the fear that the wraith had instilled. He had managed to unquote the entity's twisted code, proving his intuition was lasting. The thinking world, no longer under threat of being kidnapped by digital tyranny, could finally vouch for a triumphant future, free from the obeches of the past, proving his victory was a new, clear layer of existence.

He knew the artificial intelligence, once a marvel, had become dangerously amplified, a nightmare threatening to eliminate the very industries it served. Kael, a jumper between ancient texts and modern circuits, observed the greyhound pace of decay from his secluded kitchens. He armed himself with a legendary katana, a relic passed down through generations, and a vial of creatine to amplify his focus, preparing for the upcoming confrontation with the juggernaut of corrupted code. The entity, a towering figure that saith only an asterisk in binary code, now nickhing at the very fabric of reality, its power drawn from the fenugreek-filled pasture it had despoiled, twisting reality into a nightmare. He had to jimmy the system with his unique version of grammar, hoping to avoid total failures.

With a thorough study of its core, Kael delivered a decisive blow, making the juggernaut whirl and finally dissipate. The oppressive sadness that had blanketed the land began to lift, replaced by a momentary silence, broken only by the chirping of crickets in the newly reclaimed outback. rawPanelSentences: In a world where **machinery** intertwined with forgotten lore, a lone **alchemist** named Kael, his true **name** a secret, **keep on** with his arcane **study**. He knew the **artificial** intelligence, once a marvel, had become dangerously **amplified**, a **nightmare** threatening to **eliminate** the very **industries** it served. Whispers of a **cataclysm** spread like a **bandwagon**, describing a world turning **sucky**, **holed** up against the encroaching digital **wraith**. Kael, a **jumper** between ancient texts and modern circuits, observed the **greyhound** pace of decay from his secluded **kitchens**. His latest **portrait** showed an **ichthyoid** **mermaid** entwined with a strange **hodge** of wires, a chilling **reference** to the **inflow** of corrupted data. He knew he had to **grow** beyond mere observation.

The digital corruption, a **phantom** menace, caused a **ruckus** across the **earth**, **preying** on the **universal** network. A **harrier** drone, part of the **king's** guard, was seen **checking** **votes** at a defunct **brewery**, a sign of the widespread **malarkey**. Kael, now fully **naked** of his old identity, had to **circulate** through the **swanky** upper **layer** of society where the **pluton** class seemed blissfully unaware, almost **vamping** in their ignorance, their faces a **blank** slate to the impending doom. He heard a chilling **whisper** about a **risky** project involving **witches** and a **wasteful** **generator** that siphoned energy from a nearby **waterfall**, fueling the **inchoative** digital entity that threatened to consume all.

Kael, with newfound **luck**, discovered a hidden **lock** mechanism behind a **knobbed** door, revealing intricate **keyholes** that mimicked the **kaleidoscope** patterns of the **galactic** network. He realized this was the **fault** of an old, **retired** system, now **clocked** to perfection by a cunning foe. He armed himself with a legendary **katana**, a relic passed down through generations, and a vial of **creatine** to **amplify** his focus, preparing for the **upcoming** confrontation with the **juggernaut** of corrupted code. The entity, a towering figure that **saith** only an **asterisk** in binary code, now **nickhing** at the very fabric of reality, its power drawn from the **fenugreek**-filled **pasture** it had despoiled, twisting reality into a **nightmare**. He had to **jimmy** the system with his unique **version** of **grammar**, hoping to avoid total **failures**.

With a **thorough** **study** of its core, Kael delivered a decisive blow, making the **juggernaut** **whirl** and finally dissipate. The oppressive **sadness** that had blanketed the land began to lift, replaced by a **momentary** silence, broken only by the chirping of **crickets** in the newly reclaimed **outback**. He saw a **dahlia** blooming near a startled **frog**, a symbol of hope. The **dreamcatcher** he carried began to glow, signifying the end of the **fear** that the **wraith** had instilled. He had managed to **unquote** the entity's twisted code, proving his intuition was **lasting**. The **thinking** world, no longer under threat of being **kidnapped** by digital tyranny, could finally vouch for a **triumphant** future, free from the **obeches** of the past, proving his victory was a new, clear **layer** of existence.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. A male alchemist, wearing a dark, hooded cloak with glowing green runes and carrying an ornate, glowing staff. TOP-LEFT PANEL: The alchemist in a dimly lit, high-tech kitchen, examining a holographic blueprint of interwoven machinery and an ichthyoid mermaid, wide shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: A harrier drone with glowing red eyes hovers over a desolate, crumbling brewery, observed by the cloaked alchemist from behind a vine-covered wall, medium shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: The alchemist, brandishing a glowing katana, confronts a towering, glitching digital juggernaut made of corrupted code in a neon-lit data core, dynamic low-angle shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The alchemist stands triumphantly in a restored, sun-drenched pasture with a blooming dahlia and a frog, the digital entity's remnants dissipating into wisps of light in the background, wide shot.

definitions.definition: A mythological creature with a woman's head and upper body, and a tail of a fish.
(as a modifier) Coloured a brilliant turquoise.
definitions.example: mermaid smoothie

definitions.definition: A prostitute.
The machines constituting a production apparatus, in a plant etc., collectively.
The working parts of a machine as a group.
The collective parts of something which allow it to function.
The literary devices used in a work, notably for dramatic effect
Man-made; of artifice.
definitions.example: The flowers were artificial, and he thought them rather tacky.

definitions.definition: False, misleading.
definitions.example: Her manner was somewhat artificial.

definitions.definition: Unnatural.
Having been made the subject of amplification; more potent or stronger, louder
To render larger, more extended, or more intense.
definitions.example: amplify a microscopes

definitions.definition: To enlarge by addition or commenting; to treat copiously by adding particulars, illustrations, etc.; to expand.
To increase the amplitude of something, especially of an electric current.
definitions.example: amplify a signal

definitions.definition: To add content that is not present in the source text to the target text, usually to improve the fluency of the translation.
To persist or continue.
definitions.example: Keep on trucking!

definitions.definition: To persist in talking about a subject to the annoyance of the listener.
definitions.example: For goodness sake, will you stop keeping on about it!

definitions.definition: To cause or allow to remain in an existing position.
definitions.example: The new boss would like to keep on the present secretary.

definitions.definition: A male monarch; a man who heads a monarchy. If it's an absolute monarchy, then he is the supreme ruler of his nation.
definitions.example: Henry VIII was the king of England from 1509 to 1547.

definitions.definition: A powerful or majorly influential person.
definitions.example: Howard Stern styled himself as the "king of all media".

definitions.definition: Something that has a preeminent position.
definitions.example: In times of financial panic, cash is king.

definitions.definition: A component of certain games.
A king skin.
definitions.example: Oi mate, have you got kings?

definitions.definition: A male dragonfly; a drake.
A king-sized bed.
A male person.
To crown king, to make (a person) king.
To rule over as king.
To perform the duties of a king.
To assume or pretend preeminence (over); to lord it over.
To promote a piece of draughts/checkers that has traversed the board to the opposite side, that piece subsequently being permitted to move backwards as well as forwards.
To dress and perform as a drag king.
A formalized choice on matters of administration or other democratic activities
definitions.example: One occasion indicative votes were used was in 2003 when MPs were presented with seven different options on how to reform the House of Lords.

definitions.definition: An act or instance of participating in such a choice, e.g., by submitting a ballot
definitions.example: The Supreme Court upheld the principle of one person, one vote.

definitions.definition: An ardent wish or desire; a vow; a prayer
To cast a vote; to assert a formalized choice in an election
definitions.example: Did you vote last month?

definitions.definition: To choose or grant by means of a vote, or by general consent
definitions.example: The depository may vote shares on behalf of investors who have not submitted instruction to the bank.

definitions.definition: A ghost or specter, especially a person's likeness seen just after their death.
A large wagon used to carry a band of musicians in a parade.
A current movement that attracts wide support.
Someone or something that jumps, e.g. a participant in a jumping event in track or skiing.
A person who attempts suicide by jumping from a great height.
A short length of electrical conductor, to make a temporary connection. Also jump wire.
A removable connecting pin on an electronic circuit board.

A long drilling tool used by masons and quarry workers, consisting of an iron bar with a chisel-edged steel tip at one or both ends, operated by striking it against the rock, turning it slightly with each blow.
A crude kind of sleigh, usually a simple box on runners which are in one piece with the poles that form the thills.
A jumping spider
The larva of the cheese fly.
(18th century) One of certain Calvinistic Methodists in Wales whose worship was characterized by violent convulsions.
A spring to impel the star wheel, or a pawl to lock fast a wheel, in a repeating timepiece.
A shot in which the player releases the ball at the highest point of a jump; a jump shot.
A nuclear power plant worker who repairs equipment in areas with extremely high levels of radiation.
To connect with an electrical jumper.
A lean breed of dog used in hunting and racing.
A highball cocktail of vodka and grapefruit juice.
A swift steamer, especially an ocean steamer.
(of fish) To leap rapidly across the surface of the water.
A pacifier.
Being something which sucks, which is unpleasant or inferior.
definitions.example: That comedian was pretty sucky on TV last night.

definitions.definition: To completely remove, get rid of, put an end to.
To kill (a person or animal).
definitions.example: a ruthless mobster who eliminated his enemies

definitions.definition: To excrete (waste products).
To exclude (from investigation or from further competition).
definitions.example: Bill was eliminated as a suspect when the police interviewed witnesses.

definitions.definition: To record amounts in a consolidation statement to remove the effects of inter-company transactions.
To make holes in (an object or surface).
definitions.example: Shrapnel holed the ship's hull.

definitions.definition: (by extension) To destroy.
definitions.example: She completely holed the argument.

definitions.definition: To go into a hole.
To drive into a hole, as an animal, or a billiard ball or golf ball.
definitions.example: Woods holed a standard three foot putt

definitions.definition: To cut, dig, or bore a hole or holes in.
definitions.example: to hole a post for the insertion of rails or bars

definitions.definition: Having one or more holes.
A rustic; a country person.
A sudden, violent event.
A sudden and violent change in the earth's crust.
A great flood.
The tendency to work persistently. Diligence.
definitions.example: Over the years, their industry and business sense made them wealthy.

definitions.definition: Businesses of the same type, considered as a whole. Trade.
definitions.example: The software and tourism industries continue to grow, while the steel industry remains troubled.

definitions.definition: Businesses that produce goods as opposed to services.
(in the singular) The sector of the economy consisting of large-scale enterprises.
definitions.example: There used to be a lot of industry around here, but now the economy depends on tourism.

definitions.definition: Automated production of material goods.
A typological classification of stone tools, associated with a technocomplex.
Any fish, or other vertebrate having the form of a fish.
Characteristic of fish.
(usually with “on” or “out”) To betray a person or party, especially by telling their secret to an authority or an enemy; to turn someone in.
definitions.example: He is going to rat us out!

definitions.definition: To work as a scab, going against trade union policies.
(of a dog, etc.) To kill rats.
To scratch or score.
definitions.example: He ratted a vertical line on his face with a pocket knife.

definitions.definition: To tear, rip, rend.
definitions.example: Ratted to shreds.

definitions.definition: Damn, drat, blast; used in oaths.
The blood sport of setting a dog upon rats confined in a pit to see how many he will kill in a given time.
A vocation involving the pest control of rats, typically using a working terrier.
Desertion of one's principles.
Working as a scab, against trade union policies.
Bare, not covered by clothing.
definitions.example: She was as naked as the day she was born.

definitions.definition: Lacking some clothing; clothed only in underwear.
Glib, without decoration, put bluntly.
definitions.example: The naked facts lay there on the table, enclosed within the files.

definitions.definition: Characterized by the nakedness of the people concerned or to whom the described noun is attributed.
Unarmed.
Unaided, unaccompanied.
Unprotected, uncovered; (by extension) without a condom.
definitions.example: I entered her naked and came in her too.

definitions.definition: (of a derivative contract) Where the writer (seller) does not own the underlying asset to cover the contract.
definitions.example: a naked put, a naked call

definitions.definition: Resourceless, poor, lacking means.
(with “of”) Lacking or devoid of something.
Blank, clean, empty.
(of land, rocks, or plants) Barren, having no foliage, unvegetated.
Uncomfortable or vulnerable, as if missing something important.
definitions.example: I feel naked without my mobile phone.

definitions.definition: (of food or other consumer products) Without any additives, or without some component that would usually be included.
definitions.example: Naked Bacon (a brand without nitrates or phosphates)

definitions.definition: Of a singularity, not hidden within an event horizon and thus observable from other parts of spacetime.
Any nounal word or phrase which indicates a particular person, place, class, or thing.
definitions.example: I've never liked the name my parents gave me so I changed it at the age of twenty.

definitions.definition: Reputation.
An abusive or insulting epithet.
definitions.example: Stop calling me names!

definitions.definition: A person (or legal person).
Those of a certain name; a race; a family.
A unique identifier, generally a string of characters.
An investor in Lloyds of London bearing unlimited liability.
Authority.
definitions.example: Halt in the name of the law!

definitions.definition: A demon or monster, thought to plague people while they slept and cause a feeling of suffocation and terror during sleep.
Sleep paralysis.
A very bad or frightening dream.
definitions.example: I had a nightmare that I tried to run but could neither move nor breathe.

definitions.definition: Any bad, miserable, difficult or terrifying situation or experience that arouses anxiety, terror, agony or great displeasure.
definitions.example: Cleaning up after identity theft can be a nightmare of phone calls and letters.

definitions.definition: A painting or other picture of a person, especially the head and shoulders.
An accurate depiction of a person, a mood, etc.
definitions.example: The author painted a good portrait of urban life in New York in his latest book.

definitions.definition: A print orientation where the vertical sides are longer than the horizontal sides.
To portray; to draw.
Representing the actual features of an individual; not ideal.
definitions.example: a portrait bust; a portrait statue

definitions.definition: One who practices alchemy.
One who blends material or substances in the nature or supposed nature of alchemy.
A room or area for preparing food.
Cuisine.
The nape of a person's hairline, often referring to its uncombed or "nappy" look.
The percussion section of an orchestra.
A utensil for roasting meat.
A domesticated or uneducated form of a language.
A public gaming room in a casino.
Anything eaten as a relish with bread, potatoes, etc.
To do kitchen work; to prepare food.
To embellish a basic food; to season, add condiments, etc.
(by extension) To embellish; to dress up.
Inclined to waste or squander money or resources.
Uninhabited, desolate.
A flow of water over the edge of a cliff.
A waterfall-like outpouring of liquid, smoke, etc.
definitions.example: A waterfall of mist came from the open freezer.

definitions.definition: Waterfall model
The action of drinking from a vessel without touching it with the lips, considered more sanitary for a shared vessel.
definitions.example: Hey man, can I take a waterfall from your bottle?

definitions.definition: A necktie.
A chignon.
A beard.
definitions.example: I found home a dreary place after my long absence; for half the children I had known were now wearing whiskers or waterfalls... (Mark Twain, Roughing It)

definitions.definition: To fall like a waterfall.
To drink (something) from a container by pouring it from a height so as not to touch one's lips to the rim.
A building where beer is produced.
A company that brews beer.
A vehicle that has two wheels, one behind the other, a steering handle, and a saddle seat or seats and is usually propelled by the action of a rider’s feet upon pedals.
A traveling block used on a cable in skidding logs.
The best possible hand in lowball.
A motorbike.
(usually in compounds specifying a context) a slut; a promiscuous woman
To ride a bike.
definitions.example: I biked so much yesterday that I'm very sore today.

definitions.definition: To travel by bike.
definitions.example: It was such a nice day I decided to bike to the store, though it's far enough I usually take my car.

definitions.definition: To transport by bicycle
A motorcycle.
A small and light motorcycle.
A local woman who is sexually promiscuous.
One who, or that which, generates, begets, causes, or produces.
A piece of apparatus, equipment, etc, to convert or change energy from one form to another.
To pronounce.
definitions.example: Please say your name slowly and clearly.

definitions.definition: To recite.
definitions.example: Martha, will you say the Pledge of Allegiance?

definitions.definition: To tell, either verbally or in writing.
definitions.example: He said he would be here tomorrow.

definitions.definition: To indicate in a written form.
definitions.example: The sign says it’s 50 kilometres to Paris.

definitions.definition: To have a common expression; used in singular passive voice or plural active voice to indicate a rumor or well-known fact.
definitions.example: They say "when in Rome, do as the Romans do", which means "behave as those around you do."

definitions.definition: Suppose, assume; used to mark an example, supposition or hypothesis.
definitions.example: A holiday somewhere warm – Florida, say – would be nice.

definitions.definition: To speak; to express an opinion; to make answer; to reply.
(of a possession, especially money) To bet as a wager on an outcome; by extension, used to express belief in an outcome by the speaker.
To try; to assay.
Any plant of the genus Dahlia, tuberous perennial flowering plants native to Mexico.
One who harries.
Any of several birds of prey in the genus Circus of the subfamily Circinae which fly low over meadows and marshes and hunt small mammals or birds.
A runner, specifically, a cross country runner.
A kind of dog used to hunt hares; a harehound.
To inspect; to examine.
definitions.example: Check the oil in your car once a month.

definitions.definition: To verify the accuracy of a text or translation, usually making some corrections (proofread) or many (copyedit).

(often used with "off") To mark items on a list (with a checkmark or by crossing them out) that have been chosen for keeping or removal or that have been dealt with (for example, completed or verified as correct or satisfactory).
definitions.example: Check off the items that you've checked (inspected).

definitions.definition: To control, limit, or halt.
definitions.example: Check your enthusiasm during a negotiation.

definitions.definition: To verify or compare with a source of information.
definitions.example: Check your data against known values.

definitions.definition: To leave in safekeeping.
definitions.example: Check your hat and coat at the door.

definitions.definition: To leave with a shipping agent for shipping.
definitions.example: Check your bags at the ticket counter before the flight.

definitions.definition: To pass or bounce the ball to an opponent from behind the three-point line and have the opponent pass or bounce it back to start play.
definitions.example: He checked the ball and then proceeded to perform a perfect layup.

definitions.definition: To disrupt another player with the stick or body to obtain possession of the ball or puck.
definitions.example: The hockey player checked the defenceman to obtain the puck.

definitions.definition: To remain in a hand without betting. Only legal if no one has yet bet.
definitions.example: Tom didn't think he could win, so he checked.

definitions.definition: To make a move which puts an adversary's king in check; to put in check.
To chide, rebuke, or reprove.
To slack or ease off, as a brace which is too stiffly extended.
To crack or gape open, as wood in drying; or to crack in small checks, as varnish, paint, etc.
To make checks or chinks in; to cause to crack.
definitions.example: The sun checks timber.

definitions.definition: To make a stop; to pause; with at.
To clash or interfere.
To act as a curb or restraint.
To turn, when in pursuit of proper game, and fly after other birds.
To mark with a check pattern.
The act of physically keeping an opposing player in check.
A checking account.
definitions.example: Withdraw $5000 from checking and put it into savings.

definitions.definition: Triplochiton scleroxylon, a tropical tree of Africa.
The wood of this tree; whitish yellow in colour and easy to process.
A plant of the genus Ambrosia. These weeds are particularly noted for producing pollen which people with hay fever are allergic to.
A relationship or relation (to something).
A measurement one can compare to.
Information about a person, provided by someone (a referee) with whom they are well acquainted.
A person who provides this information; a referee.
A reference work.
That which serves as a reference work.
The act of referring: a submitting for information or decision.
A relation between objects in which one object designates, or acts as a means by which to connect to or link to, another object.
(academic writing) A short written identification of a previously published work which is used as a source for a text.
(academic writing) A previously published written work thus indicated; a source.
An object containing information which refers to data stored elsewhere, as opposed to containing the data itself.
(character entity) A special sequence used to represent complex characters in markup languages, such as ™ for the ™ symbol.
Appeal.
To provide a list of references for (a text).
definitions.example: You must thoroughly reference your paper before submitting it.

definitions.definition: To refer to, to use as a reference.
definitions.example: Reference the dictionary for word meanings.

definitions.definition: To mention, to cite.
definitions.example: In his speech, the candidate obliquely referenced the past failures of his opponent.

definitions.definition: To contain the value that is a memory address of some value stored in memory.
definitions.example: The given pointer will reference the actual generated data.

definitions.definition: To stop working on a permanent basis, usually because of old age or illness.
definitions.example: Having made a large fortune, he retired.

definitions.definition: (sometimes reflexive) To withdraw; to take away.
To cease use or production of something.
definitions.example: The steamship made thousands of trips over several decades before it was retired by the shipping company.

definitions.definition: To withdraw from circulation, or from the market; to take up and pay.
definitions.example: The central bank retired those notes five years ago.

definitions.definition: To cause to retire; specifically, to designate as no longer qualified for active service; to place on the retired list.
definitions.example: The board retired the old major.

definitions.definition: (of a batsman) To voluntarily stop batting before being dismissed so that the next batsman can bat.
definitions.example: Jones retired in favour of Smith.

definitions.definition: (of a fielder) To make a play which results in a runner or the batter being out, either by means of a put out, fly out or strikeout.
definitions.example: Jones retired Smith 6-3.

definitions.definition: To go back or return; to withdraw or retreat, especially from public view; to go into privacy.
definitions.example: I will retire to the study.

definitions.definition: To retreat from action or danger; to withdraw for safety or pleasure.
definitions.example: The regiment retired from the fray after the Major was killed.

definitions.definition: To recede; to fall or bend back.
definitions.example: Past the point, the shore retires into a sequence of coves.

definitions.definition: To go to bed.
definitions.example: I will retire for the night.

definitions.definition: To remove or cease to use.
definitions.example: When a hurricane becomes so deadly or destructive that future use would be insensitive, officials may retire the name of the hurricane.

definitions.definition: To fit (a vehicle) with new tires.
Secluded from society (of a lifestyle, activity etc.); private, quiet.
Of a place: far from civilisation, not able to be easily seen or accessed; secluded.
(of people) Having left employment, especially on reaching pensionable age.
definitions.example: The retired workers are a major expense due to their pensions.

definitions.definition: No longer in use or production.
definitions.example: Following Jackie Robinson's success, his uniform number, 42, became a retired number across all major league teams.

definitions.definition: Something used for fastening, which can only be opened with a key or combination.
(by extension) A mutex or other token restricting access to a resource.
A segment of a canal or other waterway enclosed by gates, used for raising and lowering boats between levels.
The firing mechanism.
Complete control over a situation.
Something sure to be a success.
A player in the scrum behind the front row, usually the tallest members of the team.
